special_mechanics.name: No Jump Mechanic
special_mechanics.description: Mario cannot jump due to the weight of the hammer being carried. Vertical traversal is limited to climbing ladders and falling.

special_mechanics.name: Ladder Climbing
special_mechanics.description: Mario can climb destructible and indestructible ladders for vertical movement. Optimal ladder destruction order is critical for level completion.

special_mechanics.name: Hammer Mechanics
special_mechanics.description: Mario wields a large hammer to destroy walls, pillars, and destructible ladders. The hammer weight prevents jumping.

special_mechanics.name: Grid-Based Movement
special_mechanics.description: The playfield is divided into an invisible grid where each space can contain one object. Movement appears constrained to this grid system.

tier_notes: Tier 2 empirical/qualitative data. Available documentation focuses on gameplay mechanics and level design rather than detailed physics parameters. No numerical movement values, gravity constants, or sub-pixel precision systems are documented in the Data Crystal RAM map or Wikipedia sources. The game's distinctive feature is the absence of jump mechanics, making traditional jump physics parameters not applicable.
sources_used: data_crystal - Wrecking Crew page (ROM specifications, mapper info)
wikipedia - Wrecking Crew (video game) page (gameplay mechanics)
